prompt:
CI fix for PR #568. The build-home jobs (paflynn@bushmills, paflynn@crostini, patrick@crostini, personal) fail with:

    error: The option `cosmo.gemini' does not exist. Definition values:
    - In '.../home/identities/work.nix': { enable = false; }

CAUSE: This PR removed the `cosmo.gemini.enable` option from home/dev.nix, but `home/identities/work.nix` still ASSIGNS it. Line 3 of home/identities/work.nix contains:

    cosmo.gemini.enable = false;

This orphaned assignment now references a non-existent option, breaking evaluation of every home config that imports the work identity (the work/standalone home-manager hosts). The nixos hosts use the personal identity and don't set it, which is why only build-home failed.

FIX (surgical):
1. Remove the line `cosmo.gemini.enable = false;` from home/identities/work.nix. Remove just that line cleanly; leave the rest of the file intact and valid (make sure the surrounding attrset still parses — if it leaves an empty/awkward block, tidy it minimally).
2. Re-grep the ENTIRE repo to be sure nothing else references `cosmo.gemini` or `gemini-cli` (git grep -nE 'cosmo\\.gemini|gemini-cli'). The only remaining 'gemini' hit anywhere should be the unrelated `gemini-code-assist[bot]` trusted-reviewer line in home/dev.nix — leave that alone.
3. Validate it evaluates now. Importantly, the failing configs are the standalone homeConfigurations, so check one of those specifically, e.g.:
     nix eval .#homeConfigurations."paflynn@bushmills".activationPackage.drvPath
   (and/or `nix flake check`). Confirm no 'cosmo.gemini' error remains.

Commit on the SAME branch (feat/antigravity-native) with a focused message like `fix(home): drop orphaned cosmo.gemini assignment in work identity`. Follow repo conventions: 2-space indent; do NOT add any 'Co-Authored-By' line and do NOT mention Claude, AI, LLM, or assistant in the commit message or PR.
